session_summary: Investigated and fixed pollution of the resume-session named-session registry. Found that subprocess Claude invocations from the ai-semantic-source-scanner SAST pipeline and Ghost Security scan skills were auto-registering on exit, polluting ~/.claude/session-names/index.json with 29,531 junk entries. Pruned the registry, added a CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER env-var opt-out mechanism in both the SessionEnd hook (consumer) and all five ai-codescan pipeline subprocess sites (producers), then merged the summarize-context and claude-session-organizer skill repos into a single tracked repo with the handoff file now written to the git repo root and auto-committed on the current branch.
key_topics: handoff file path = git repo root

objective.primary: Stop subprocess Claude invocations from polluting the named-session registry and consolidate session-related skills into one tracked repo so the handoff round-trips across machines.
explicit_constraints: Don't lose the AI_Codescan named session
Force-push to claude-session-organizer was explicitly authorized after pushback
Auto-commit the handoff to the current branch (option 1 of 4) so it travels via normal git push/pull
implicit_assumptions: The user is fine with feature branches carrying chore: update claude session handoff commits; they can squash or skip in PRs
Pre-commit hooks on the user's repos can be bypassed for the background auto-commit (--no-verify)
Signing follows whatever the user's git config dictates; no explicit bypass

outcome.summary: Registry pruned from 29,697 entries to 166 (backup at ~/.claude/session-names/index.json.bak-1778497896). AI_Codescan session intact (id fdeedac4-d9f3-4a7e-b3f8-df862fee3287). CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER=1 honored by the SessionEnd hook and set by five ai-codescan call sites. summarize-context and claude-session-organizer merged: force-pushed to pwnpanda/claude-session-organizer main, local directory renamed, symlinks repointed. .context-handoff.json now writes to git repo root and the hook auto-commits it on the current branch with safety guards (skip rebase/merge in progress, detached HEAD, gitignored file, unchanged file).
outcome.value_provenance: Subagent runs no longer pollute the named-session registry, and the per-project conversation handoff travels with the project via normal git push/pull.

files_created.purpose: New skill subdirectory in the merged repo holding the resume-session skill (formerly its own claude-session-organizer flat repo).
This file. Manual /conversation-summary invocation at the user's request.

files_modified.change_summary: Add env["CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER"] = "1" before bash loop.sh subprocess (line 226).
Same env-var setter before bash loop.sh subprocess (line 193).
Same env-var setter before bash loop.sh subprocess (line 127).
Same env-var setter before bash loop.sh subprocess (line 118).
Same env-var setter for the storage_taint_resolver subprocess (line 1032).
Honor CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER=1: skip the auto-register path when set; touch (refresh) of already-registered sessions still runs.

Write .context-handoff.json to git repo root (fallback cwd). Export CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER=1 before --bare child invocation. Add maybe_commit_handoff() that auto-commits the file on the current branch via git commit --no-verify --only -m '...' -- .context-handoff.json, with skip guards for rebase/merge/cherry-pick/bisect, detached HEAD, gitignored file, unchanged content.
Multi-skill installer: now also links resume-session skill and the /save command, and merges three hooks (SessionEnd x2 + UserPromptSubmit).
Mirror of install.sh: removes the three skills' symlinks and hook entries.
Read handoff from git repo root first, fall back to cwd.
Manual-invocation section now points at <repo-root>/.context-handoff.json with cwd fallback; description string updated.
Rebranded around claude-session-organizer, documents three skills, repo-root output path, auto-commit, and the CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER subagent contract.

commands_executed.command: session_registry.py prune-equivalent (manual Python script with backup + atomic write)
commands_executed.purpose: Remove 29,531 pipeline-subagent auto entries while keeping 22 manual + 144 real auto entries.
Overwrite claude-session-organizer history with merged tree containing all three skills.
Rename local dir after merge. Old claude-session-organizer dir moved to /tmp/trash/old-claude-session-organizer-1778506844.
Repoint ~/.claude/skills/{conversation-summary,load-context,resume-session} and ~/.claude/commands/save.md to the new path; hooks already present and normalized.
Clean up leftover remote so origin is the only one.
Confirm env-var additions in ai_codescan don't break orchestration tests.

external_resources.purpose: Destination repo for the merged skill tree (force-pushed).
Now-orphaned source repo; user will archive manually.

decisions.decision: Merge summarize-context INTO claude-session-organizer (overwrite history) rather than the reverse
decisions.rationale: User explicitly asked for this direction after pushback flagged that summarize-context already had multi-skill layout (less restructuring). User chose 'easiest merge, then move project over to claude-session-organizer, overwrite history'.
alternatives_rejected: Merge claude-session-organizer into summarize-context and retire claude-session-organizer
Keep both repos and only sync the CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER patch

decisions.decision: Use a CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER env-var contract between callers and the SessionEnd hook
decisions.rationale: Subprocess Claude invocations cannot easily be distinguished from interactive sessions by the hook alone, so an explicit opt-out is more reliable than heuristics on session length or prompt content.
alternatives_rejected: Prompt-content matching in the hook (also fragile)

decisions.decision: Write .context-handoff.json at git repo root (fallback cwd), and auto-commit it on the current branch
decisions.rationale: The handoff travels with the project rather than being pinned to the subdirectory the session happened to start in, and normal git push/pull makes it available on every machine without extra plumbing.
alternatives_rejected: Sidecar branch (e.g. claude-handoff) to keep feature branches clean — user chose option 1
Manual commit only (zero invasiveness but relies on user remembering)

decisions.rationale: Auto-commit runs in a background, disowned subshell triggered by SessionEnd. Pre-commit hooks designed for interactive workflows would block the commit and the user would never know. The commit only touches an auxiliary JSON file, so bypassing lint/test hooks is justified.
alternatives_rejected: Run pre-commit hooks (would frequently block the auto-commit)
Ask the user every time (defeats the point)

knowledge_acquired.fact: 29,389 entries had the prefix read-and-follow-* from Ghost Security scan-code planner/analyzer subagents; 142 entries had nominator-iteration-* from ai-codescan's wide_nominator skill.

Five places spawn `bash loop.sh` which exec `claude -p ...`: nominator.py:227, validator.py:206, analyzer.py:128, engines/llm_heavy.py:118, cli.py:1039 (storage_taint_resolver). All build env dicts; injecting CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER=1 there propagates to the child.

--bare on `claude --print` skips ALL hooks in the child process, so the SessionEnd hook from resume-session would not fire there anyway. The CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER env var in write_summary.sh is defense-in-depth.
`git commit --only -- file -m 'msg'` is wrong: -- ends option parsing, so -m is treated as a pathspec. Correct order: `git commit --only -m 'msg' -- file`.

User's ~/.claude/hooks/rm_to_trash.py intercepts `rm -rf` and trashes ALL paths that look like rm targets in the command — including paths separated by `&&`. Recovery from /tmp/trash worked, but use single-target commands when calling rm to avoid collateral.

errors_encountered.error: rm_to_trash hook moved /home/robin/git/priv/summarize-context/resume-session/scripts/ to /tmp/trash along with the intended __pycache__ subdirectory.
errors_encountered.context: Trying to remove __pycache__ after a cp -r that included it.
errors_encountered.resolution: mv /tmp/trash/1778506281-scripts back into place; scripts intact.

errors_encountered.error: git commit auto-commit logic initially failed with 'error: pathspec -m did not match any file(s)'.
errors_encountered.context: First draft of maybe_commit_handoff() in write_summary.sh placed -m after -- in the git commit invocation.
errors_encountered.resolution: Reordered to `git commit --no-verify --only -m '...' -- .context-handoff.json`. Re-tested across new/unchanged/modified/detached/gitignored scenarios — all pass.

errors_encountered.error: Five test scenarios in the smoke test for maybe_commit_handoff initially mis-tested the gitignored case because the file had already been committed in earlier scenarios and gitignore was therefore inactive for it.
errors_encountered.resolution: Re-tested in a fresh repo where the file was never tracked; check-ignore correctly returned non-zero and the skip path triggered.

unanswered_questions: Whether to archive github.com/pwnpanda/summarize-context (it is now orphaned; force-push obliterated its sibling claude-session-organizer history but summarize-context's own GitHub repo is untouched).
unfinished_work: Five files in ai-semantic-source-scanner have uncommitted modifications adding env["CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER"] = "1" — nominator.py, validator.py, analyzer.py, engines/llm_heavy.py, cli.py. The user has not yet asked for these to be committed in ai-semantic-source-scanner.
This .context-handoff.json itself is being added and pushed to ai-semantic-source-scanner per the user's most recent request; the commit and push are in flight at the time of writing.

interaction_dynamics.roles_and_personas: User: senior engineer, blunt and autonomy-friendly per stored memory; spotted design holes (registry pollution) quickly. Claude: tactical executor; gave one round of pushback on the merge direction before proceeding.

tone_fragments.notes: User opens with the symptom (lost AI_Codescan name); Claude investigates and presents two distinct problems.
User picks prune scope and prevention strategy via AskUserQuestion; Claude executes without further questions.
User asked for a non-trivial repo merge in a direction with real structural friction. Claude pushed back; user revised plan; Claude executed.
Cleanup: auto-commit feature, remote pruning, manual /conversation-summary.

drift_or_contradictions: Turn 8: user initially asked to merge summarize-context INTO claude-session-organizer; after pushback in turn 9, user flipped direction (merge claude-session-organizer INTO summarize-context, then move/rename) — net result identical but the layout migration changed sides.
interaction_dynamics.annotation_notes: User asked for pushback when warranted ('Give pushback if that doesn't work as well or if there is a reason to prefer the other solution'). Claude provided one round of pushback and the user revised. No sarcasm or hidden intent detected.

user_signals.communication_style: terse, decisive, comfortable with destructive operations when explicitly authorized
preferences_observed: Wants Claude to make calls and only ask when there's real ambiguity
Comfortable with force-push when explicitly authorized; expects pushback if a plan has structural friction
Tracks side effects: noticed when symlinks would break, expected install.sh to be re-run
Prefers tactical end-of-turn summaries (what changed + what's next) over decorative ones
feedback_given: 'I think we do the opposite. Merge summarize-context into claude-session-organizer. Give pushback if that doesn't work as well' — explicit invitation to challenge direction
'Lets do the easiest merge, then move the whole project over to claude-sessio-organizer and overwrite git history or push brand new state.' — destructive operation explicitly authorized
'set the remote to git@github.com:pwnpanda/claude-session-organizer.git so the repo works normally' — wanted the leftover summarize-context-origin remote removed

prompting_strategies.techniques_observed: AskUserQuestion with preview blocks for visual comparison of branching strategies; one round of explicit pushback with bullet-list of pros/cons before proceeding with a destructive operation.
micro_prompts_used: Pushback frame: present 2 concrete reasons + recommendation; let user keep or flip course in one round.
Confirm-before-destruct frame: state exact destructive command sequence, get explicit 'yes' before executing.
prompting_strategies.model_adaptations: Kept terse-by-default; spent words on pushback and rationale only when the user-stated path had real friction.

context_artifacts.tool_notes: AskUserQuestion preview blocks (single-select only) used to compare branch-history layouts. Bash tool's `rm` is intercepted by ~/.claude/hooks/rm_to_trash.py with naïve path extraction that can over-collect targets — recoverable via /tmp/trash.

handoff.context_brief: The user owns three Claude Code skill repos that have just been consolidated into one: pwnpanda/claude-session-organizer now contains conversation-summary, load-context, and resume-session skills. pwnpanda/summarize-context is orphaned (force-push obliterated its sibling history; the source repo is untouched but no longer used). The resume-session named-session registry at ~/.claude/session-names/index.json has been pruned from 29,697 entries to 166; AI_Codescan and the user's other manually-named sessions are intact. Five files in this repo (ai-semantic-source-scanner) have uncommitted modifications that add env["CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER"] = "1" to the five subprocess sites that spawn `claude` as a pipeline subagent (nominator, validator, analyzer, llm_heavy engine, storage_taint_resolver in cli). Tests still pass. The conversation-summary SessionEnd hook now writes .context-handoff.json to the git repo root and auto-commits it on the current branch with safety guards. This file is the manual mid-session checkpoint requested by the user.
what_next_session_should_know: Five files in ai_codescan/ have uncommitted CLAUDE_NO_AUTO_REGISTER=1 changes — not committed yet; user may want them on a separate commit from the handoff.
Backup of the polluted registry is at ~/.claude/session-names/index.json.bak-1778497896. Delete after a few sessions confirm the new state is stable.
summarize-context-origin remote was removed from claude-session-organizer; if user wants to push a tombstone commit to the orphaned summarize-context GitHub repo, they'd need to re-add it.
The auto-commit uses --no-verify; if any future pre-commit hooks should run on handoff commits, the maybe_commit_handoff function in write_summary.sh needs editing.
The pipeline tests at /home/robin/git/priv/ai-semantic-source-scanner/tests/ run via .venv/bin/python -m pytest (project-local venv, not the linuxbrew Python on PATH).
